description: CLI pre-call input layer: normalize flag forms, derive arrayKey from config, and accept the scripts' input shorthands so the CLI reaches script-parity on INPUT without changing in-pi ops. All edits live in cli.ts's pre-call path (parseOpArgs ~:184-233 + the required-filter exemption ~:293-303). Closes FGAP-019 (arrayKey required-but-derivable across 7 block-mutation ops — derive from config.block_kinds[].array_key, demote --arrayKey to optional override by extending the required-filter exemption from {writer} to {writer,arrayKey} + an injectArrayKey helper mirroring injectWriter), FGAP-025 (--writer kind:id, --where field:op:value, CSV --op in shorthands, each a pre-call transform in parseOpArgs), FGAP-064 (kebab-case flag normalization — a kebab->camel alias layer in parseOpArgs so conventional --dry-run / --id forms resolve to the camelCase op-schema keys), and FGAP-032 (the id-flag instance of the same root — --id aliases to the op's single declared id-param: --itemId/--parentId/--taskId/--unitId — resolved in the SAME normalization layer as FGAP-064). The ops still receive + require their declared params; the CLI normalizes/supplies them. Additive per the FEAT-008 invariant: every currently-working flag form keeps working, the op surface is byte-unchanged.
acceptance_criteria: FGAP-019 closed: append/update/upsert/remove-block-item (+ nested parents) accept --block without --arrayKey; the op is byte-unchanged (still receives + requires arrayKey, the CLI supplies it)
FGAP-025 closed: --writer kind:id, --where field:op:value, and CSV --op in parse to the op's declared param shape before invocation
FGAP-064 closed: conventional kebab-case flags (e.g. update --dry-run) resolve to the camelCase op-schema key; the existing camelCase form still works
FGAP-032 closed: --id resolves to the op's single declared id-param across id-taking ops (find-references --id no longer mis-fires)
In-pi op behavior byte-unchanged (frozen-ops constraint honored)
Canonical pipeline: build/check/test + runtime demo (drive the CLI directly) + fresh adversarial audit before green
notes: Release-group: cli-parity (FEAT-008) | order 1/6 | addresses FGAP-019, FGAP-025, FGAP-064, FGAP-032 | category: CLI-pre-call (R-0003) | flag-normalization + shorthands + arrayKey all edit parseOpArgs -> one task per feature-decomposition rule 4
